title: SB 1254 (The FRESH Act) connects incarcerated individuals with vital services such as CalFresh and other resources prior to release in order to increase the success of re-entry.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 1254 (The FRESH Act) connects incarcerated individuals with vital services such as CalFresh and other resources prior to release in order to increase the success of re-entry.",
    "The FRESH Act allows and assists incarcerated individuals to pre-apply for CalFresh benefits 90 days prior to their release.",
    "The FRESH Act taps into underutilized federal funding that supports CalFresh and expands access for individuals that would otherwise qualify for it.",
    "Food insecurity is one of the most challenging hurdles that previously-incarcerated individuals face upon re-entry.",
    "In 2019, roughly 1 in 5 formerly incarcerated people suffered from food insecurity.",
    "The FRESH Act creates a process for access to CalFresh and other supportive services for formerly incarcerated individuals.",
    "Removing barriers to necessary services will increase the likelihood of a successful re-entry for previously-incarcerated individuals.",
    "The FRESH Act helps reduce food insecurity in California."
  ]
}
```
